reasoning: C had the most genuinely distinct five because two forms escape the document frame entirely: the Artifact Reliquary replaces paper with a physical box of objects (failed prototype, erased page, the rock), and the Negative Space Map reports only what the school deliberately did NOT teach, while the Reciprocal Card flips who assesses whom. B is nearly as strong and the most systematically spread, naming a different broken assumption per version, with the Forward Contract (a report in no past tense, a negotiated wager) and the Hundred-Year Card (a different measurement clock) being conceptually rigorous standouts. A is crafted and vivid (Question Museum, Unofficial Biography) but clusters slightly, since Friction Map and Contribution Ledger occupy adjacent territory and all five stay safely within written-document-about-the-child, taking the least format-level risk.
